programmes.show_description: 16/29. Four chefs compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?
1/6. When the circumstances of a crime novelist's death eerily mirror the details of his final novel, Humphrey must uncover whether it was a coincidence or murder by design.
6/6. Tensions rise with Mammy, igniting an emotional confrontation between the sisters. Contains some strong language.
Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.
6/12. Roman Kemp recommends exercise where you can gain without the pain.
40/50. Denise Nurse helps a couple who want a unique Sussex home, and the quirkier, the better! Her line-up includes some wow-factor properties, any one of which could be the mystery house.
Monty is busy with spring and planning ahead for summer. He's doing a seed-sowing masterclass, cutting back Cornus and dividing ferns to put in the new Woodland Garden.
Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.
29/30. Game show in which players score points by answering questions correctly while avoiding the Impossible answers.
14/25. In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches. Hosted by Alex Scott.
4/25. Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be all right. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot?
12/15. In his search for la dolce vita (the sweet life), Clive discovers why an osteria is superior to a pub, how to make perfect pasta and the dark history of an 800-year-old tower.
3/3. Monkey Planet discovers how primates are individuals with a sense of self and why brainpower is essential to primate survival.
18/26. Michael Aspel and experts examine curios and artefacts offered up by the public. In this edition, the team return to Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire.
18/76. Paul Martin is joined by antique experts Tracy Martin and Charlie Ross at the magnificent Sheldonian Theatre in the centre of historic Oxford.
4/100. Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.
4/15. In Northern Ireland, Rick has Filipino-style Sunday lunch and meets a 'no waste' chef turning an unpopular fish into a stunning dish. At home, he cooks beef and Guinness pie.
17/29. The remaining three chefs from Northern Ireland compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?
6/12. Zara is shattered by a shocking discovery on her cattle round, Noah misses lambing his Herdwicks while studying for school exams, and Gayle is signed off work with pregnancy concerns.
A passenger flight from London to Belfast ends in motorway horror. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.
10/12. Angela Scanlon and guests take a deep dive into the candidates' performances.
Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Joe Pike.   Followed by Weather.

programmes.show_name: Clash of the Superpowers: America vs China
programmes.show_description: 2/2. As the battle for technological supremacy ramps up, a controversial visit to Taiwan brings the two superpowers closer to a dangerous confrontation.

programmes.show_name: Inside Britain's National Parks - New Forest
programmes.show_description: 1/4. A look at the New Forest, a mosaic of heath, ancient woodlands, coastline and villages.

5/10. Mary begins to flourish in London society, but a run-in with an old rival and troubling news from home threaten her growing confidence and force her to face up to the past.
6/10. Mary rushes to Pemberley, where she is swept back into her family's orbit. It feels as if Mary is right back where she started - until a visit from Mr Ryder shakes things up.

7/12. Clive Myrie meets soon-to-be-grandmother Tracey and suggests to her that borrowing a pet might lower her blood pressure.
Martel and Nitro are duelling with each other over ideas at a studio flat in London. Martin is in Somerset, where another character property is ripe for reinvention.
Dean restores a pair of shoes with a surprising past, Mark revives a retro recording device, and Sonnaz and Angelina pair up to salvage a battered survivor of heady days in London.
Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.
Julie Hesmondhalgh and her niece Rosa present an appeal on behalf of Ovarian Cancer Action, a charity funding vital research to improve early diagnosis and develop better treatments.
Colourful dramatisation of the birth, life and death of Christ, depicting the tensions between Herod's court and the Roman administration, and the execution of John the Baptist. [1961]

5/10. Coast ventures to the farthest-flung reaches of the British Isles. Mark Horton reveals how Brunel's ship the Great Eastern conquered the Atlantic.

Monty is on hand with wheelbarrows of practical gardening advice and inspiration, while Ashley Edwards heads to a magical spring garden in Horsham to share his love of the season of renewal.
